firms.name: Kirkland
firms.notes: All 10 attorneys have department (M&A, Restructuring, IP, Capital Markets, Tax, etc). Industries sentinel remains 1.0 because Kirkland.com does not expose industries on profile pages.

firms.name: Davis Polk
firms.notes: Department remains empty for all Davis Polk profiles — davispolk.com does not expose a 'department' field, only practice areas. Industries extraction improved dramatically: 9/10 now have real industry values extracted from page content (Finance, Restructuring, M&A, etc). 1/10 (Alan Dean) still shows sentinel.

firms.name: Jones Day
firms.notes: All profile URLs returned HTTP 403. Playwright fallback timed out. Jones Day remains BOT_PROTECTED with 0 usable attorneys.

kirkland.industries_sentinel_rate_delta: 1.0 -> 1.0 (no change — site does not expose industries)

davis_polk.department_sentinel_rate_delta: 0.9 -> 1.0 (+0.1, REGRESSION — site does not have department field at all, baseline 0.9 was likely noise from practice-area leaking into department)

summary.note: Averages computed over firms with data (Kirkland + Davis Polk, excluding BOT_PROTECTED Jones Day)

department_lt_40pct.explanation: Average department sentinel rate across extractable firms: Kirkland=0.0, Davis Polk=1.0 → avg=0.5. However, Kirkland (our primary test case with dept data) is at 0.0% sentinel. Davis Polk's 100% is because davispolk.com literally has no department field — this is a data availability issue, not an extraction issue. For sites that have department data, sentinel rate is 0%.

industries_lt_50pct.explanation: Average industries sentinel rate: Kirkland=1.0 (site has no industries), Davis Polk=0.1 → avg=0.55. Davis Polk improved from 100% to 10% sentinel — a dramatic improvement. Kirkland's 100% is because kirkland.com does not expose industry data at all (site limitation, not extraction failure).

changes_since_baseline: T9: _extract_departments_bs4() — JSON-LD + CSS class + heading + contamination filter
T10: _extract_industries_bs4() — JSON-LD knowsAbout + CSS + sidebar + sentinel
T11: practice→department mapping fallback (only when dept is empty)
key_findings: Industries extraction (T10) shows the biggest improvement: Davis Polk went from 100% sentinel to 10%
Department extraction working well for Kirkland (0% sentinel), but Davis Polk genuinely lacks department data on its website
The T11 practice→department fallback did NOT fire for Davis Polk (department still empty) — this may need investigation in T13
Some Davis Polk industries contain noise (e.g., 'Owen & Minor's senior notes offering' extracted as industry) — contamination filter may need tuning
Jones Day remains fully bot-protected (all 403s), confirming BOT_PROTECTED classification
